symbols.description: Small bullet/dot punctuation marks used between script tokens

drawings.description: Crude line drawing depicting a stick-figure humanoid on the left holding what appears to be a stick/tool, pointing toward a large spiral or concentric circular form in the center-right. Above the spiral is a second smaller figure (possibly winged or with extended limbs) that appears to be falling or flying. Dashed lines and small marks surround the figures, possibly indicating motion, ground, or impact lines.

mixed_media_regions.description: Text section with bullet/separator punctuation marks embedded throughout the constructed script

uncertain: The script language could not be identified — it appears to be a constructed/fictional writing system, possibly resembling a fantasy language (e.g., Klingon-like, Tolkien-inspired, or a custom cipher). Common visual features include circular glyphs with dots, angular brackets, and 'Y'-shaped and 'X'-shaped characters.
Exact letter-by-letter transcription of the constructed script is not possible without a known key/cipher.
Specific meaning and identity of the figures in the drawing (the humanoid, the spiral, the upper falling/flying figure) is unclear; may be allegorical or symbolic.
Whether the bullet/separator dots are part of the script itself or editorial punctuation is unknown.
